agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

### agent_supports: yes

Aider supports a non-interactive, single-shot turn via the `--message`/`-m` flag: it processes one natural-language instruction, applies any edits, and exits — no interactive chat session. See https://aider.chat/docs/scripting.html. This is exactly the `headless_mode` capability the `basic-turn` primitive requires (`replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` → `headless_mode: true`). The canonical recipe drives aider interactively in a tmux REPL (a drift fix: a headless `--message` run exits before the IdleFlush settle fires), but the primitive being exercised — transcript turn-end detection — still anchors on `headless_mode`.

The `basic-turn` recipe prompt — "Reply with exactly the word: ok" — is a pure one-message round-trip with no tool use (`tool_calls_max: 0`), which is the smallest turn aider can run. Aider persists every exchange to `.aider.chat.history.md` in the project CWD (https://aider.chat/docs/faq.html) and prints a `> Tokens: X sent, Y received.` line after each model call (e.g. `Tokens: 7.7k sent, 167 received`), confirmed across the aider docs and HISTORY. So a clean single turn produces a fully-formed transcript with real token usage.

### daemon_capability: full

The aider adapter is `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", Parser: RawLineParser}` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/agent.go`). Walking the two User-observable signals against the parser as it exists today:

1. **`ready → working → ready`** — observed correctly. The `#### ` user-prompt line emits a `user_message` event (`parser.go` `ParseLineRaw`). The SessionDetector force-bounces a ready session to working when fresh activity arrives with a non-empty `LastEventType` (`session_detector_activity.go:343-350`, `ForceReadyToWorkingReason`), so working is entered. Aider's idle TUI prompt never lands in the markdown history, so there is no in-band turn-end marker; the parser synthesizes `turn_done` out-of-band via `IdleFlush` once the transcript has been quiet for `aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter` (1500ms). `turn_done` is the authoritative end-of-turn signal (`session.go` `IsAgentDone`), flipping the session back to ready. The final state is `ready` and the topology is exactly `ready → working → ready`.
2. **`TotalTokens > 0`** — observed correctly. The `> Tokens:` line is matched by `tokensRE` and routed through `closeModelCall`, which emits an `assistant_message` carrying a `TokenSnapshot{Input, Output, Total}`. `applyTokenSnapshot` (`tailer_metrics.go:119-125`) sets `metrics.TotalTokens = tokens.Total` whenever `Total > 0`. For the recipe's `> Tokens: 771 sent, 1 received.` this yields `TotalTokens = 772`.

Both spec-required observables (the topology and the non-zero token count, per `scenarios.json → verify`) are emitted correctly by the parser today, so the daemon is `full`. The one wrinkle — the working→ready edge is synthesized from quiescence rather than an in-band marker, and lands with ~5s effective latency (1500ms IdleFlush floor clamped to the SessionDetector's 5s working-refresh poll, documented at `parser.go:42-54`) — affects only TIMING, not state correctness. Latency is not a `verify` assertion, so it is a caveat, not a `bug`: there is no spec-required observable the daemon mis-handles, and the trace fully exists in the watched Source.

### driver_capability: ready

The interactive driver (`scripts/drive-aider-interactive.sh`) implements `send`, `wait_turn`, `slash`, `interrupt`, and `sleep`. The `basic-turn` recipe is the canonical happy path: one `send` ("Reply with exactly the word: ok"), one `wait_turn` (blocks on the next `> Tokens:` line), then an 8s `sleep` to cover the IdleFlush + working-refresh settle before teardown. Every step type the recipe needs is implemented, so the driver is `ready` — no gapped primitive (restart/reset_session/resume/start_session/exit_clean/sigkill/keys/session/fork) is touched by this recipe.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: aider's idle TUI prompt is never written to .aider.chat.history.md, so there is no in-band turn-end marker. The parser synthesizes turn_done via IdleFlush instead — the state arc is correct, but the end-of-turn is inferred from quiescence rather than observed.

metric-drift-downstream: the working→ready transition lands with ~5s effective latency (1500ms IdleFlush floor clamped to the SessionDetector's 5s working-refresh poll), so the session shows as working for several seconds after aider has finished. State correctness is unaffected; only the transition timing is delayed.
The recipe uses the same trailing-sleep settle as the sibling full-lifecycle-toolcall cell (>=8s) so the synthesized working→ready edge lands before the tmux kill-session teardown.

sources.note: --message/-m one-shot non-interactive mode: process one instruction, apply edits, exit — the headless_mode capability the primitive requires
aider writes chat history to .aider.chat.history.md in the project directory
aider prints '> Tokens: X sent, Y received.' per model call; total tokens sent/received tracked
Source = FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser} — per-project markdown transcript; no PendingContributor/SubagentCounter

#### → user_message; > Tokens: → assistant_message w/ TokenSnapshot; no in-band turn-end → IdleFlush synthesizes turn_done (1500ms floor at lines 42-54); ~5s effective working→ready latency documented there — proves the latency caveat, not a bug
applyTokenSnapshot (lines 119-125): metrics.TotalTokens = tokens.Total when Total > 0 — emits the TotalTokens>0 signal correctly (daemon_capability:full)
IsAgentDone: LastEventType == turn_done is the authoritative end-of-turn → flips working to ready
lines 343-350: force ready→working on fresh activity so ClassifyState can later detect the working→ready edge
TestParser_BaselineHello_FullTurn (lines 35-118): uses the exact basic-turn recipe prompt; pins assistant_message+TokenSnapshot on > Tokens: and turn_done from IdleFlush
driver implements send/wait_turn/slash/interrupt/sleep — basic-turn needs only send + wait_turn + sleep, all present → driver_capability:ready
headless_mode: true — the Primitive exercised maps to this capability key (confirmed)

preconditions: aider CLI on PATH and authenticated against its configured provider/model (auth is set up out-of-band; the rig's precheck delegates auth to the user)
tmux available — the interactive driver runs aider in a tmux REPL so it stays alive across the turn
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
setup: The interactive driver (drive-aider-interactive.sh) git-inits a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/, launches `aider --no-auto-commits --yes-always --no-gitignore` in a tmux session, and waits for the `> Repo-map:` banner line in .aider.chat.history.md before sending the prompt

aider stores its transcript as .aider.chat.history.md in the cwd (FilesUnderCWD), so the daemon keys the whole session by proc-<PID> — there is no separate transcript-UUID rollout session; the turn arc, pid_discovered and teardown all land on that one proc-<PID> row

WHY interactive, not headless `aider --message` (drift fix, same shape as the sibling full-lifecycle-toolcall + pi cells): aider emits no end-of-turn marker, so working→ready is synthesized by IdleFlush after a ~1.5s quiet window, clamped to the SessionDetector's ~5s working-refresh poll; a headless --message run exits ~2.6s after the working flip — BEFORE that settle fires — so the daemon never observes the terminal working→ready edge and turn_end + teardown can't anchor (the prior 3/5 result). Keeping aider alive in the REPL across one send + wait_turn lets the daemon observe the full ready→working→ready settle cleanly, and the single tmux kill-session teardown then yields one clean process_exited

wait_turn blocks until a new `> Tokens:` line appears in the transcript (aider's per-round close marker), so the trailing sleep never races a still-running turn; the 8s trailing sleep covers aider's ~1.5s IdleFlush quiet window plus the 5s working-refresh poll (one full idle-flush cycle plus slack) so the working→ready settle is observed before teardown

The send is a no-tool single turn — aider is asked to reply with exactly one word, so the working span carries no auto-executed tool call (tool_calls_max: 0) and the session never enters waiting (aider has no permission hook)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl contains a transcript_new + presession_created for the proc-<PID> session born ready within ~1s of REPL launch
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event bound to the same proc-<PID> session_id
events.jsonl contains a state_transition→working on the proc-<PID> session_id when the prompt is sent (the single assistant turn)

events.jsonl contains a state_transition→ready on the same proc-<PID> session_id once aider's turn-end is inferred (the `> Tokens:` line closes the model call and IdleFlush synthesizes turn_done after ~1.5s of quiescence, clamped to the 5s working-refresh poll) — observed within the 8s trailing sleep before teardown
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to waiting for the session at any point (no tool call, no permission gate)
the session's final live state is ready before the driver tears the REPL down
events.jsonl contains exactly one process_exited on the proc-<PID> session at REPL teardown
